steps.title: State the information purpose
actions: Name the before and after states.
Describe what continuity, causality, feedback, hierarchy, or narrative pacing the motion must communicate.
Reject arbitrary motion; allow expressive or narrative motion when the thesis declares its role and it remains non-blocking.
exit_criteria: The animation has a specific information job or is removed.

steps.title: Gate opportunities by frequency and task cost
actions: Map how often each candidate interaction occurs and how much attention, latency, and movement it adds.
Keep only candidates with a named feedback, continuity, state, jarring-change, or explanatory purpose.
Record representative rejected candidates, especially frequent keyboard workflows and functional data that users are reading or acting on.
exit_criteria: The motion budget is concentrated on a bounded set of high-leverage moments, and restraint is evidenced by explicit rejections.

steps.title: Compose a small motion grammar
actions: Name two to four reusable roles such as focal entry, move, feedback, emphasis, or exit.
Map each role to a high-value moment and define origin, easing, duration, interruption, and reduced behavior.
Keep ordinary reading content stable rather than wrapping every section in the same reveal effect.
exit_criteria: The direction has a repeatable cadence without making every element compete for attention.

steps.title: Define state, focus, and interruption behavior
actions: Specify trigger, duration bounds, reversal, cancellation, rapid repeated input, route change, and cleanup.
Define focus ownership before, during, and after.
Ensure business state does not depend on animation completion and that interruption begins from the live presentation value rather than a stale target.
exit_criteria: Every interruption lands in the latest valid application state.

steps.title: Specify direct manipulation and gesture physics
actions: For drag, swipe, scrub, or reorder behavior, define the grab offset, pointer capture, cancellation, direction lock, bounds, snap targets, multi-pointer policy, and keyboard alternative.
Track the pointer continuously and decide whether release needs live velocity handoff, momentum projection, or progressive boundary resistance.

Treat published curves, thresholds, and spring values as starting hypotheses; tune them on real hardware and exclude momentum from consequential or precision interactions when it reduces predictability.
exit_criteria: The interaction remains continuous, reversible, predictable, and task-complete across supported input modes.

steps.title: Design the reduced-motion equivalent
actions: Identify vestibular, automatic, repeated, and attention-demanding movement.
Create an instant, fade, or static equivalent that preserves information.
Define pause controls for ongoing motion.
exit_criteria: The reduced path preserves every task, state, and piece of information.

steps.title: Implement from geometry and tokens
actions: Use bounded duration, consistent easing, and spatial origins tied to the trigger.
Prefer compositor-friendly properties when they preserve correct geometry and semantics, but do not promote excessive layers or repeat performance folklore without measurement.
Adapt paths when layout geometry changes and keep gesture-driven motion synchronized to the live rendered value.
exit_criteria: Motion feels connected to the interaction at every supported geometry.

steps.title: Stress-test input and performance
actions: Spam triggers, reverse intent, resize, navigate, cancel pointers, add a second pointer, and toggle preferences mid-animation.
Profile long frames, layout, paint, and layer memory on representative low-power hardware.
Use slow-motion or frame-by-frame playback for coordinated properties and test gestures on physical touch hardware.
Test script and API fallback.
exit_criteria: Final visual, DOM, focus, and ARIA states agree under stress and remain within budget.

steps.title: Review motion in the full experience
actions: Evaluate simultaneous animations and page-level cadence.
Inventory shared tokens and primitives, group findings by root cause and leverage, and distinguish code-observable defects from runtime feel judgments.
Confirm the selected expressive direction has enough recurring motion roles to feel intentional, then remove repeated reveal patterns and competing focal moments.
For audit-only work, produce self-contained plans with exact scope, values, boundaries, revision, and feel checks; for authorized fixes, rerun the same audit after implementation.
Run default and reduced paths through completion gates.
evidence: self-contained fix plans or verified implementation
exit_criteria: Motion supports comprehension without delaying reading, interaction, or release readiness.
